response: Charles A. Agemian had more career changes than Arshile Gorky. Agemian retired from Chase Manhattan Bank early and became chairman and CEO of Hackensack Trust Co., which later became Garden State National Bank. He did not have a significant career change in terms of artistic or professional direction, but his career path involved a transition from banking to management. Arshile Gorky, on the other hand, was a prominent artist whose career was largely focused on painting and did not involve significant professional transitions. Therefore, based on the provided context, **Charles A. Agemian had more career changes**.
